rec.title: Today's Recommendation
rec.recovery.name: Today is a recovery day
rec.recovery.descRun: Loosen up with a 30-min recovery jog.
rec.recovery.descSwim: Easy swim + drills, keep it light.
rec.recovery.descBike: Rest or do a light Z1 spin to flush the legs.
rec.recovery.advice.0: Adequate sleep and hydration accelerate recovery.
rec.recovery.advice.1: 15 min of stretching or foam rolling recommended.
rec.endurance.descBike: Pedal steadily in Z2 for 2 hours.
rec.endurance.adviceSwim: Focus on stroke efficiency and hold pace.
rec.endurance.adviceOther: Avoid letting heart rate climb into Z3.
rec.tempo.descRun: Stimulate threshold with a 40-min tempo run.
rec.tempo.descBike: Stimulate aerobic capacity with Z3 intervals.
rec.tempo.advice.0: Run the main set after a 15-min warmup.
rec.tempo.advice.1: Get adequate protein after the session.
rec.fatigue.name: Accumulated fatigue — recovery first
rec.fatigue.desc: Skip the workout and let your body recover.
rec.fatigue.advice.0: Recovery is more valuable than training today.

triView.emptyChart: Log activities to see the chart
triView.header.title: Are my triathlon disciplines balanced?
triView.header.subtitle: 3-discipline CTL stack · Total CTL = Σ(bTSS + rTSS + sTSS)
triView.pmc.title: Performance Management Chart · 3-Discipline Stack
triView.pmc.sub: 90d actual + 91d projected · Stacked areas = Cycling + Running + Swimming CTL
triView.goal.distance: 1.5 km swim + 40 km bike + 10 km run
triView.model.title: Integrated Load Model — Calculation
triView.model.desc: Total CTL = Cycling CTL + Running CTL + Swimming CTL (simple sum). Each discipline's TSS is computed independently as a 42-day EWMA, and the summed total CTL represents overall fitness. TSB (form) — the difference between CTL and ATL (7-day average) — is positive when recovered and negative when fatigued.
triView.contrib.title: Discipline Contribution
triView.contrib.advice: Cycling is currently {{pct}}% of your load. Use the recommended Olympic distance ratio (40/35/25) as a guide to balance your disciplines.
triView.contrib.adviceDemo: Cycling is the largest contributor at 45%. Swimming is 3pp below the recommended Olympic ratio (40/35/25) — adding 20–30 swim TSS per week would rebalance the load.

ranking.footerFallback: Cohort sample too small → vs all {{n}} riders · updated weekly
pacing.title: Outdoor pacing guide
pacing.note: Long-ride target — −10 to −5% of threshold power (CP {{cp}}W). For flat cruising / gran-fondo pacing.

riderType.title: Rider type · capability & tendency
riderType.cogganNote: Coggan power profile (male, v1 · gender/age not applied)
riderType.type.RoadSprinter.label: Road Sprinter
riderType.type.RoadSprinter.desc: Explosive finisher with strong sprint power (5s–1m).
riderType.type.TrackSprinter.label: Track Sprinter
riderType.type.TrackSprinter.desc: Dominant raw power; wins on absolute output over W/kg.
riderType.type.AllRounder.label: All-Rounder
riderType.type.AllRounder.desc: Balanced in sprint, sustained, and power-to-weight. Few weaknesses.
riderType.type.Puncher.label: Puncher
riderType.type.Puncher.desc: Explosive on short climbs and repeated intervals (1–5 min).
riderType.type.Climber.label: Climber
riderType.type.Climber.desc: Light rider with high sustained W/kg. Strong on long climbs.
riderType.type.TimeTrialist.label: Time Trialist
riderType.type.TimeTrialist.desc: Strong 20min+ sustained power. Optimal for flat solo efforts and TTs.
riderType.type.Unclassified.label: Unclassified
riderType.type.Unclassified.desc: Not enough power data to classify. Ride more at varied intensities.
